suggestions.topic: glamsterdam-devnet-7 launch readiness and scoping confirmation
suggestions.rationale: devnet-7 was targeted for early last week (July 14) but hasn't been mentioned since. Need to confirm launch status, address any blocking issues, and verify EIP-7688 (progressive containers), EIP-7928 (block-access-list changes), and EIP-8282 (builder contracts) are all ready.

suggestions.topic: Early August public Gloas devnet planning and scope
suggestions.rationale: Public/Mekong-style devnet for app testing was targeted for early August (now ~2 weeks away). Need to finalize scope, timeline, coordination with app teams, and ensure devnet-7 stability first.

suggestions.topic: Beacon API PR #625 (ProduceBlock v4 auth/config) stakeholder feedback
suggestions.rationale: Urgent feedback was requested from staking pools, builders, DVT providers, and VC tooling on July 9. This is a critical API change for ePBS and blocks finalization of the builder workflow.
Deferred from ACDC #179 (May 28) with explicit 'two weeks' revisit target. Authors stated it's already implemented and tested since devnet-1, making this an overdue inclusion decision.

suggestions.topic: Hegota non-headliner EIP submissions and review process
suggestions.rationale: Submission window closes August 6 (17 days away). Need to review any new proposals, confirm process, and begin scoping discussions for Hegota's feature set.

Targeted for devnet-7 in June, but DFI decision was explicitly deferred pending devnet-6 stability. devnet-6 has been running ~80% for weeks; time to make a firm inclusion/exclusion call for Glamsterdam mainnet.

suggestions.topic: ePBS circuit breaker implementation status and standardization
suggestions.rationale: Teku and Lodestar have implemented builder-index-aware logic; Prysm is taking a different approach with trusted-builder fallback. Potuz mentioned a forthcoming document. Need to check document status and determine if any standardization is needed.

suggestions.topic: Client implementation readiness for devnet-7 features
suggestions.rationale: As of July 9, Prysm/Nimbus/Lodestar were passing tests, Teku needed EOD update, Grandine needed a few days. Confirm all clients are ready and identify any remaining blockers.
